instruction: Design a Go gRPC service with protobuf best practices including field numbering strategy, oneof for polymorphism, well-known types, and backward-compatible schema evolution.
output:
<think>
Protobuf schema design has lasting consequences since it's a wire format contract. Key principles: (1) Field numbers 1-15 use 1 byte for the tag, so reserve them for frequently accessed fields. Numbers 16-2047 use 2 bytes. Never reuse deleted field numbers - use 'reserved' to prevent accidental reuse. (2) Oneof is protobuf's union type - great for polymorphic messages like events or responses that can be one of several types. Each field in a oneof shares memory, so only one can be set. (3) Well-known types (google.protobuf.Timestamp, Duration, FieldMask, Struct) should be used instead of reinventing them. Timestamp is better than int64 for time because it has standard serialization. FieldMask enables partial updates (like PATCH). (4) Schema evolution rules: never change field numbers or types of existing fields, use 'reserved' for removed fields, add new fields with new numbers, use 'optional' for new fields to distinguish unset from default. For Go specifically, proto3 optional generates pointer fields which let you distinguish zero value from unset.
</think>

```protobuf
// api/v1/service.proto
syntax = "proto3";

package api.v1;

import "google/protobuf/timestamp.proto";
import "google/protobuf/duration.proto";
import "google/protobuf/field_mask.proto";
import "google/protobuf/struct.proto";

option go_package = "github.com/myapp/api/v1;apiv1";

// Reserve deleted fields to prevent reuse
message User {
  reserved 6, 8;          // deleted: phone, legacy_role
  reserved "phone", "legacy_role";

  // Fields 1-15: hot path (1-byte tag)
  string id = 1;
  string email = 2;
  string display_name = 3;
  UserStatus status = 4;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp created_at = 5;
  repeated string roles = 7;

  // Fields 16+: less frequent (2-byte tag)
  optional string bio = 16;  // optional => pointer in Go
  map<string, string> metadata = 17;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp updated_at = 18;
}

enum UserStatus {
  USER_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED = 0;  // Always have 0 = unspecified
  USER_STATUS_ACTIVE = 1;
  USER_STATUS_SUSPENDED = 2;
  USER_STATUS_DELETED = 3;
}

// Oneof for polymorphic events
message Event {
  string event_id = 1;
  google.protobuf.Timestamp occurred_at = 2;
  string actor_id = 3;

  oneof payload {
    UserCreated user_created = 10;
    UserUpdated user_updated = 11;
    UserDeleted user_deleted = 12;
    OrderPlaced order_placed = 13;
  }
}

message UserCreated { User user = 1; }
message UserUpdated {
  User user = 1;
  google.protobuf.FieldMask updated_fields = 2;
}
message UserDeleted { string user_id = 1; string reason = 2; }
message OrderPlaced { string order_id = 1; int64 amount_cents = 2; }

// Partial update with FieldMask
message UpdateUserRequest {
  User user = 1;
  google.protobuf.FieldMask update_mask = 2;
}

service UserService {
  rpc GetUser(GetUserRequest) returns (User);
  rpc UpdateUser(UpdateUserRequest) returns (User);
  rpc ListUsers(ListUsersRequest) returns (ListUsersResponse);
  rpc WatchEvents(WatchEventsRequest) returns (stream Event);
}

message GetUserRequest { string id = 1; }
message ListUsersRequest {
  int32 page_size = 1;
  string page_token = 2;
  string filter = 3;
}
message ListUsersResponse {
  repeated User users = 1;
  string next_page_token = 2;
  int32 total_count = 3;
}
message WatchEventsRequest {
  google.protobuf.Timestamp since = 1;
  repeated string event_types = 2;
}
```

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	apiv1 "github.com/myapp/api/v1"
	"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/fieldmaskpb"
	"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb"
)

// Handling FieldMask for partial updates
func applyFieldMask(existing, update *apiv1.User, mask *fieldmaskpb.FieldMask) {
	for _, path := range mask.GetPaths() {
		switch path {
		case "display_name":
			existing.DisplayName = update.DisplayName
		case "email":
			existing.Email = update.Email
		case "bio":
			existing.Bio = update.Bio
		case "status":
			existing.Status = update.Status
		case "metadata":
			existing.Metadata = update.Metadata
		default:
			log.Printf("Unknown field mask path: %s", path)
		}
	}
	existing.UpdatedAt = timestamppb.Now()
}

// Processing polymorphic events via oneof
func processEvent(ctx context.Context, event *apiv1.Event) error {
	switch p := event.Payload.(type) {
	case *apiv1.Event_UserCreated:
		log.Printf("User created: %s", p.UserCreated.User.Email)
	case *apiv1.Event_UserUpdated:
		log.Printf("User updated: fields=%v",
			p.UserUpdated.UpdatedFields.GetPaths())
	case *apiv1.Event_UserDeleted:
		log.Printf("User deleted: %s reason=%s",
			p.UserDeleted.UserId, p.UserDeleted.Reason)
	case *apiv1.Event_OrderPlaced:
		log.Printf("Order placed: %s $%.2f",
			p.OrderPlaced.OrderId,
			float64(p.OrderPlaced.AmountCents)/100)
	default:
		log.Printf("Unknown event type at %v", event.OccurredAt.AsTime())
	}
	return nil
}
```

instruction: Implement a Go gRPC server-side and bidirectional streaming service for real-time data processing with flow control, backpressure handling, and proper stream lifecycle management.
output:
<think>
Streaming in gRPC has several patterns: server-streaming (server sends many responses), client-streaming (client sends many, server responds once), and bidirectional (both stream). For real-time data processing, bidirectional is most flexible. Key concerns: (1) Flow control - gRPC uses HTTP/2 flow control under the hood, but at the application level we need to handle slow consumers. If the server produces faster than the client can consume, we need backpressure. In Go, this manifests as stream.Send() blocking when the buffer is full. We should add a send timeout. (2) Proper lifecycle: detect client disconnect via context cancellation, handle EOF on recv, send trailer metadata with stats. (3) Error handling: distinguish between client cancellation (codes.Canceled), deadline exceeded, and actual errors. (4) Backpressure pattern: use a channel between producer goroutine and the send loop, with a bounded buffer. If the channel fills up, we can either drop messages (lossy) or block the producer (lossless). For data processing pipelines, we want lossless with a reasonable buffer and timeout.
</think>

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"net"
	"sync"
	"sync/atomic"
	"time"

	"google.golang.org/grpc"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/metadata"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/status"
)

// Simulated protobuf messages
type DataPoint struct {
	ID        string
	Value     float64
	Timestamp int64
}

type ProcessedResult struct {
	InputID   string
	Result    float64
	LatencyMs int64
}

type StreamStats struct {
	Processed int64
	Dropped   int64
	Errors    int64
}

type DataProcessorServer struct {
	maxBufferSize int
	processTimeout time.Duration
}

func NewDataProcessorServer() *DataProcessorServer {
	return &DataProcessorServer{
		maxBufferSize:  1000,
		processTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
	}
}

// Server-streaming: client sends query, server streams results
func (s *DataProcessorServer) StreamResults(
	req *DataPoint,
	stream interface{ Send(*ProcessedResult) error; Context() context.Context },
) error {
	ctx := stream.Context()
	var sent int64

	for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
		select {
		case <-ctx.Done():
			log.Printf("Client disconnected after %d results", sent)
			return status.Error(codes.Canceled, "client disconnected")
		default:
		}

		result := &ProcessedResult{
			InputID:   req.ID,
			Result:    req.Value * float64(i+1),
			LatencyMs: 1,
		}

		// Send with timeout for backpressure detection
		sendDone := make(chan error, 1)
		go func() { sendDone <- stream.Send(result) }()

		select {
		case err := <-sendDone:
			if err != nil {
				return status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "send failed: %v", err)
			}
			atomic.AddInt64(&sent, 1)
		case <-time.After(s.processTimeout):
			log.Printf("Send timeout - client too slow")
			return status.Error(codes.DeadlineExceeded, "client too slow")
		case <-ctx.Done():
			return status.Error(codes.Canceled, "client disconnected")
		}
	}

	// Send trailer metadata with stats
	trailer := metadata.Pairs("x-results-sent", fmt.Sprintf("%d", sent))
	grpc.SetTrailer(stream.Context(), trailer)
	return nil
}

// Bidirectional streaming with worker pool and backpressure
type BidiStream interface {
	Send(*ProcessedResult) error
	Recv() (*DataPoint, error)
	Context() context.Context
}

func (s *DataProcessorServer) ProcessStream(stream BidiStream) error {
	ctx := stream.Context()
	stats := &StreamStats{}

	// Buffered channel for backpressure
	inputCh := make(chan *DataPoint, s.maxBufferSize)
	resultCh := make(chan *ProcessedResult, s.maxBufferSize)
	errCh := make(chan error, 1)

	var wg sync.WaitGroup

	// Receiver goroutine
	wg.Add(1)
	go func() {
		defer wg.Done()
		defer close(inputCh)
		for {
			data, err := stream.Recv()
			if err == io.EOF {
				log.Println("Client finished sending")
				return
			}
			if err != nil {
				if st, ok := status.FromError(err); ok && st.Code() == codes.Canceled {
					return
				}
				select {
				case errCh <- err:
				default:
				}
				return
			}
			select {
			case inputCh <- data:
			default:
				// Buffer full - backpressure
				atomic.AddInt64(&stats.Dropped, 1)
				log.Printf("Backpressure: dropping message %s", data.ID)
			}
		}
	}()

	// Worker pool
	numWorkers := 4
	for i := 0; i < numWorkers; i++ {
		wg.Add(1)
		go func(workerID int) {
			defer wg.Done()
			for data := range inputCh {
				start := time.Now()
				// Process data
				result := &ProcessedResult{
					InputID:   data.ID,
					Result:    data.Value * 2.0,
					LatencyMs: time.Since(start).Milliseconds(),
				}
				select {
				case resultCh <- result:
					atomic.AddInt64(&stats.Processed, 1)
				case <-ctx.Done():
					return
				}
			}
		}(i)
	}

	// Close results when workers done
	go func() {
		wg.Wait()
		close(resultCh)
	}()

	// Sender loop
	for result := range resultCh {
		if err := stream.Send(result); err != nil {
			log.Printf("Send error: %v", err)
			atomic.AddInt64(&stats.Errors, 1)
			break
		}
	}

	log.Printf("Stream complete: processed=%d dropped=%d errors=%d",
		stats.Processed, stats.Dropped, stats.Errors)

	select {
	case err := <-errCh:
		return status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "stream error: %v", err)
	default:
		return nil
	}
}

func main() {
	lis, err := net.Listen("tcp", ":50051")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("Failed to listen: %v", err)
	}
	s := grpc.NewServer()
	log.Printf("gRPC server listening on :50051")
	if err := s.Serve(lis); err != nil {
		log.Fatalf("Failed to serve: %v", err)
	}
}
```

instruction: Write Go gRPC interceptors for unary and streaming calls that provide structured logging, metrics collection, panic recovery, and request ID propagation.
output:
<think>
Interceptors are gRPC's middleware mechanism. In Go, there are 4 types: unary server, stream server, unary client, stream client. For server-side interceptors, we need: (1) Structured logging - log method, duration, status code, request ID for every call. Use slog for structured logging. (2) Metrics - track request count, latency histogram, error rate per method. We can use prometheus client_golang. (3) Panic recovery - catch panics in handlers and convert to gRPC Internal errors instead of crashing the server. This is critical for production. (4) Request ID propagation - extract request ID from incoming metadata, or generate one, and inject into context. Downstream calls should propagate it. The interceptor chain order matters: recovery should be outermost (catches panics from everything inside), then request ID (available to all inner interceptors), then logging (has request ID), then metrics. For streaming interceptors, we wrap the ServerStream to intercept SendMsg/RecvMsg. grpc.ChainUnaryInterceptor and ChainStreamInterceptor handle composition.
</think>

```go
package middleware

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"runtime/debug"
	"sync/atomic"
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
	"google.golang.org/grpc"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/metadata"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/status"
)

type contextKey string

const requestIDKey contextKey = "request_id"

// Simple metrics collector
type Metrics struct {
	TotalRequests  atomic.Int64
	TotalErrors    atomic.Int64
	ActiveStreams  atomic.Int64
	MethodCounts   sync.Map // method -> *atomic.Int64
	MethodLatency  sync.Map // method -> *latencyTracker
}

import "sync"

type latencyTracker struct {
	mu    sync.Mutex
	total time.Duration
	count int64
}

func (lt *latencyTracker) Record(d time.Duration) {
	lt.mu.Lock()
	lt.total += d
	lt.count++
	lt.mu.Unlock()
}

var globalMetrics = &Metrics{}

func GetRequestID(ctx context.Context) string {
	if id, ok := ctx.Value(requestIDKey).(string); ok {
		return id
	}
	return ""
}

// --- Request ID Interceptor ---

func UnaryRequestID() grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor {
	return func(ctx context.Context, req interface{}, info *grpc.UnaryServerInfo,
		handler grpc.UnaryHandler) (interface{}, error) {
		requestID := extractOrGenerateID(ctx)
		ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, requestIDKey, requestID)
		// Set response header
		grpc.SetHeader(ctx, metadata.Pairs("x-request-id", requestID))
		return handler(ctx, req)
	}
}

func StreamRequestID() grpc.StreamServerInterceptor {
	return func(srv interface{}, ss grpc.ServerStream, info *grpc.StreamServerInfo,
		handler grpc.StreamHandler) error {
		ctx := ss.Context()
		requestID := extractOrGenerateID(ctx)
		ctx = context.WithValue(ctx, requestIDKey, requestID)
		ss.SetHeader(metadata.Pairs("x-request-id", requestID))
		wrapped := &wrappedStream{ServerStream: ss, ctx: ctx}
		return handler(srv, wrapped)
	}
}

func extractOrGenerateID(ctx context.Context) string {
	if md, ok := metadata.FromIncomingContext(ctx); ok {
		if ids := md.Get("x-request-id"); len(ids) > 0 {
			return ids[0]
		}
	}
	return uuid.New().String()
}

// --- Logging Interceptor ---

func UnaryLogging(logger *slog.Logger) grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor {
	return func(ctx context.Context, req interface{}, info *grpc.UnaryServerInfo,
		handler grpc.UnaryHandler) (interface{}, error) {
		start := time.Now()
		reqID := GetRequestID(ctx)

		resp, err := handler(ctx, req)

		st, _ := status.FromError(err)
		duration := time.Since(start)

		logAttrs := []slog.Attr{
			slog.String("method", info.FullMethod),
			slog.String("request_id", reqID),
			slog.Duration("duration", duration),
			slog.String("code", st.Code().String()),
		}

		if err != nil {
			logger.LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelError, "gRPC call failed", logAttrs...)
		} else {
			logger.LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "gRPC call", logAttrs...)
		}
		return resp, err
	}
}

func StreamLogging(logger *slog.Logger) grpc.StreamServerInterceptor {
	return func(srv interface{}, ss grpc.ServerStream, info *grpc.StreamServerInfo,
		handler grpc.StreamHandler) error {
		start := time.Now()
		reqID := GetRequestID(ss.Context())

		logger.Info("gRPC stream started",
			"method", info.FullMethod,
			"request_id", reqID)

		err := handler(srv, ss)

		st, _ := status.FromError(err)
		logger.Info("gRPC stream ended",
			"method", info.FullMethod,
			"request_id", reqID,
			"duration", time.Since(start),
			"code", st.Code().String())
		return err
	}
}

// --- Panic Recovery Interceptor ---

func UnaryRecovery(logger *slog.Logger) grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor {
	return func(ctx context.Context, req interface{}, info *grpc.UnaryServerInfo,
		handler grpc.UnaryHandler) (resp interface{}, err error) {
		defer func() {
			if r := recover(); r != nil {
				stack := string(debug.Stack())
				logger.Error("Panic recovered in gRPC handler",
					"method", info.FullMethod,
					"panic", fmt.Sprintf("%v", r),
					"stack", stack,
					"request_id", GetRequestID(ctx))
				err = status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "internal server error")
			}
		}()
		return handler(ctx, req)
	}
}

func StreamRecovery(logger *slog.Logger) grpc.StreamServerInterceptor {
	return func(srv interface{}, ss grpc.ServerStream, info *grpc.StreamServerInfo,
		handler grpc.StreamHandler) (err error) {
		defer func() {
			if r := recover(); r != nil {
				logger.Error("Panic recovered in gRPC stream",
					"method", info.FullMethod,
					"panic", fmt.Sprintf("%v", r),
					"stack", string(debug.Stack()))
				err = status.Errorf(codes.Internal, "internal server error")
			}
		}()
		return handler(srv, ss)
	}
}

// --- Wrapped stream for context propagation ---

type wrappedStream struct {
	grpc.ServerStream
	ctx context.Context
}

func (w *wrappedStream) Context() context.Context { return w.ctx }

// --- Server setup with chained interceptors ---

func NewServer(logger *slog.Logger) *grpc.Server {
	return grpc.NewServer(
		grpc.ChainUnaryInterceptor(
			UnaryRecovery(logger),
			UnaryRequestID(),
			UnaryLogging(logger),
		),
		grpc.ChainStreamInterceptor(
			StreamRecovery(logger),
			StreamRequestID(),
			StreamLogging(logger),
		),
	)
}
```

instruction: Create a Go gRPC health check service implementation with dependency checking, graceful degradation, and integration with Kubernetes readiness/liveness probes.
output:
<think>
Health checking in gRPC follows the standard health checking protocol (grpc.health.v1.Health). Kubernetes uses this for readiness and liveness probes. The implementation needs: (1) A health server that tracks the status of individual services/dependencies (database, cache, external APIs), (2) Dependency checking - periodically probe each dependency and update status, (3) Graceful degradation - if a non-critical dependency is down, the service can still be SERVING but with reduced functionality, (4) Integration with K8s - liveness probe checks if the process is alive (should almost always pass unless deadlocked), readiness probe checks if the service can handle traffic (should fail if critical deps are down). The grpc_health_v1 package provides the standard implementation. We extend it with background health checkers that periodically verify each dependency. The status hierarchy: SERVING (all good), NOT_SERVING (critical dep down), UNKNOWN (starting up). We use separate service names for liveness vs readiness so K8s can target them independently.
</think>

```go
package health

import (
	"context"
	"database/sql"
	"log/slog"
	"sync"
	"time"

	"google.golang.org/grpc"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/codes"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/health"
	healthpb "google.golang.org/grpc/health/grpc_health_v1"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/status"
)

type DependencyStatus int

const (
	StatusUnknown DependencyStatus = iota
	StatusHealthy
	StatusDegraded
	StatusUnhealthy
)

type Dependency struct {
	Name     string
	Critical bool // If critical, failing makes service NOT_SERVING
	Checker  func(ctx context.Context) error
}

type HealthManager struct {
	mu           sync.RWMutex
	healthServer *health.Server
	deps         []Dependency
	statuses     map[string]DependencyStatus
	logger       *slog.Logger
	interval     time.Duration
	cancel       context.CancelFunc
}

func NewHealthManager(logger *slog.Logger, interval time.Duration) *HealthManager {
	return &HealthManager{
		healthServer: health.NewServer(),
		statuses:     make(map[string]DependencyStatus),
		logger:       logger,
		interval:     interval,
	}
}

func (hm *HealthManager) RegisterDependency(dep Dependency) {
	hm.mu.Lock()
	defer hm.mu.Unlock()
	hm.deps = append(hm.deps, dep)
	hm.statuses[dep.Name] = StatusUnknown
}

// Common dependency checkers
func DatabaseChecker(db *sql.DB) func(context.Context) error {
	return func(ctx context.Context) error {
		return db.PingContext(ctx)
	}
}

func GRPCServiceChecker(conn *grpc.ClientConn, service string) func(context.Context) error {
	return func(ctx context.Context) error {
		client := healthpb.NewHealthClient(conn)
		resp, err := client.Check(ctx, &healthpb.HealthCheckRequest{
			Service: service,
		})
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		if resp.Status != healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_SERVING {
			return status.Errorf(codes.Unavailable, "service %s: %s", service, resp.Status)
		}
		return nil
	}
}

func HTTPChecker(url string, timeout time.Duration) func(context.Context) error {
	return func(ctx context.Context) error {
		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)
		defer cancel()
		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "GET", url, nil)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		resp.Body.Close()
		if resp.StatusCode >= 500 {
			return fmt.Errorf("unhealthy status: %d", resp.StatusCode)
		}
		return nil
	}
}

import (
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
)

func (hm *HealthManager) Start(ctx context.Context) {
	ctx, hm.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
	// Set initial status
	hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_NOT_SERVING)
	hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("liveness", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_SERVING)
	hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("readiness", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_NOT_SERVING)

	go hm.checkLoop(ctx)
}

func (hm *HealthManager) Stop() {
	if hm.cancel != nil {
		hm.cancel()
	}
	hm.healthServer.Shutdown()
}

func (hm *HealthManager) checkLoop(ctx context.Context) {
	ticker := time.NewTicker(hm.interval)
	defer ticker.Stop()

	// Immediate first check
	hm.checkAll(ctx)

	for {
		select {
		case <-ticker.C:
			hm.checkAll(ctx)
		case <-ctx.Done():
			return
		}
	}
}

func (hm *HealthManager) checkAll(ctx context.Context) {
	hm.mu.Lock()
	defer hm.mu.Unlock()

	anyCriticalDown := false
	anyDegraded := false

	for _, dep := range hm.deps {
		checkCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second)
		err := dep.Checker(checkCtx)
		cancel()

		prevStatus := hm.statuses[dep.Name]
		var newStatus DependencyStatus

		if err != nil {
			newStatus = StatusUnhealthy
			if dep.Critical {
				anyCriticalDown = true
			} else {
				anyDegraded = true
			}
		} else {
			newStatus = StatusHealthy
		}

		if newStatus != prevStatus {
			hm.logger.Info("Dependency status changed",
				"dependency", dep.Name,
				"critical", dep.Critical,
				"old_status", prevStatus,
				"new_status", newStatus,
				"error", err)
		}
		hm.statuses[dep.Name] = newStatus
	}

	// Update gRPC health status
	if anyCriticalDown {
		hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_NOT_SERVING)
		hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("readiness", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_NOT_SERVING)
	} else {
		hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_SERVING)
		hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("readiness", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_SERVING)
	}
	// Liveness always SERVING unless we're deadlocked
	hm.healthServer.SetServingStatus("liveness", healthpb.HealthCheckResponse_SERVING)
}

func (hm *HealthManager) GetStatus() map[string]DependencyStatus {
	hm.mu.RLock()
	defer hm.mu.RUnlock()
	result := make(map[string]DependencyStatus, len(hm.statuses))
	for k, v := range hm.statuses {
		result[k] = v
	}
	return result
}

func (hm *HealthManager) Register(server *grpc.Server) {
	healthpb.RegisterHealthServer(server, hm.healthServer)
}
```
